note: Hreflang is a small module: one page-attachments hook, a three-key config object, a config settings form, and a config-save cache-clearing subscriber. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a multilingual Drupal 11 site, what exactly does the Hreflang module add to a page, and how does that differ from Drupal core Content Translation?
evals.expected_output: It adds <link rel="alternate" hreflang="…" href="…"> tags to every page for each enabled language, whereas core Content Translation adds hreflang tags only to translated entity pages.

evals.prompt: I want an additional hreflang="x-default" tag pointing at my site's default language. Which Hreflang setting enables it, and what config object holds it?
evals.expected_output: Enable the x_default key (default true) on the hreflang.settings config object; add the setting via the form at /admin/config/search/hreflang or drush cset hreflang.settings x_default 1.

evals.prompt: Which Hreflang setting makes the x-default tag point at the configured fallback (selected) language instead of the site default language?
evals.expected_output: The x_default_fallback key (default true) on hreflang.settings points the x-default tag at the language.negotiation selected_langcode fallback language when one is configured.

evals.prompt: How do I make Hreflang stop adding per-language tags on content entity pages and let core Content Translation handle them instead, and what is the caching trade-off?
evals.expected_output: Enable defer_to_content_translation (default false) on hreflang.settings; Hreflang then adds only the x-default tag on entity pages. Trade-off: Content Translation omits query args from its tags (invalid tag set on query-arg pages) but improves cache efficiency.

evals.prompt: What is the admin path and route for the Hreflang settings form, and which permission gates it?
evals.expected_output: The form is at /admin/config/search/hreflang (route hreflang.admin_settings, under Search and metadata), gated by the 'administer site configuration' permission.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to modify or remove the hreflang tags Hreflang adds. Hreflang has no API of its own — which core hooks do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_language_switch_links_alter() (changes the source language-switch links) or hook_page_attachments_alter() (edits the generated html_head_link entries); Hreflang provides no module-specific hook.

evals.prompt: On a single-language Drupal site, will the Hreflang module output any hreflang tags? And are tags added on 404/403 pages?
evals.expected_output: No — hreflang_page_attachments() returns early when the site is not multilingual, and it also skips 403/404 (exception) pages, so no hreflang tags are emitted in those cases.

evals.prompt: When I change Hreflang settings, why do the new hreflang tags appear immediately without a manual cache clear?
evals.expected_output: The hreflang.config_subscriber (HreflangConfigSubscriber) listens on config save and, when any of the three keys change, invalidates the http_response cache tag so page caches are rebuilt with the new settings.
